query: Could you direct me towards a study that explores the potential to predict a reader's native language based on their eye movement patterns while reading English texts?
criteria: native language prediction eye movements reading English
eye tracking L1 identification reading English
cross-linguistic eye movement patterns reading English

recommendation_reason.value: Using this methodology, we demonstrate for the first time that the native language of English learners can be predicted from their gaze fixations when reading English.

supported_research_question.value: A fundamental question in language learning concerns the role of a speaker's first language in second language acquisition.

method.value: analysis of eye-movement patterns in second language reading of free-form text

data_or_scenario.value: second language reading of free-form text

main_conclusion.value: Using this methodology, we demonstrate for the first time that the native language of English learners can be predicted from their gaze fixations when reading English.

limitations.value: All the sentences are manually annotated with part-of-speech (POS) tags and syntactic dependency trees.

relevance_strength.value: We present a novel methodology for studying this question: analysis of eye-movement patterns in second language reading of free-form text.

title.value: Assessing Language Proficiency from Eye Movements in Reading

recommendation_reason.value: We present a novel approach for determining learners' second language proficiency which utilizes behavioral traces of eye movements during reading.

supported_research_question.value: reflect the extent to which the learner's gaze patterns in reading are similar to those of native English speakers.

method.value: Our approach provides standalone eyetracking based English proficiency scores which reflect the extent to which the learner's gaze patterns in reading are similar to those of native English speakers.

data_or_scenario.value: Our approach yields the strongest performance when the test taker is presented with a suite of sentences for which we have eyetracking data from other readers.

main_conclusion.value: our results open the door to future methods for inferring reader characteristics from the behavioral traces of reading.

limitations.value: However, it remains effective even using eyetracking with sentences for which eye movement data have not been previously collected.

relevance_strength.value: We present a novel approach for determining learners' second language proficiency which utilizes behavioral traces of eye movements during reading.

title.value: Native Language Prediction from Gaze: a Reproducibility Study

recommendation_reason.value: Numerous studies found that the linguistic properties of a person's native language affect the cognitive processing of other languages. However, only one study has shown that it was possible to identify the native language based on eye-tracking records of natural L2 reading using machine learning.

data_or_scenario.value: eye-tracking records of natural L2 reading

main_conclusion.value: comparable classification performance is maintained despite using less data.

limitations.value: the correlation between L2 eye movements and native language similarity may be more complex than the original study found.
